back_translation: For the choirmaster. To the tune of 'Lilies.' A psalm of David.
O God, save me, for the waters have come up to my neck.
I have sunk into deep mire where there is no foothold; I have come into deep waters, and the current sweeps over me.
I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched; my eyes fail as I wait for my God.
Those who hate me without cause are more than the hairs on my head; many are those who would destroy me without cause; they force me to restore what I did not steal.
O God, You know my folly, and my guilt is not hidden from You.
O Lord GOD of hosts, let not those who hope in You be ashamed because of me; O God of Israel, let not those who seek You be disgraced because of me.
For I have borne scorn for Your sake; shame has covered my face.
I have become a stranger to my brothers and a foreigner to my mother's sons,
because zeal for Your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach You have fallen on me.
When I wept and fasted, it brought me reproach.
When I wore sackcloth, I became an object of mockery to them.
Those who sit at the city gate gossip about me, and I am the song of drunkards.
But as for me, O LORD, my prayer comes up to You at an acceptable time; O God, in Your abundant steadfast love, answer me with Your sure salvation.
Rescue me from the mire; do not let me sink; deliver me from those who hate me and from the deep waters.
Do not let the flood sweep over me, nor the deep swallow me, nor the Pit close its mouth over me.
Answer me, O LORD, for Your steadfast love is good; turn to me according to Your great compassion.
I am in distress; answer me quickly; do not hide Your face from Your servant.
Draw near to my soul and redeem me; ransom me because of my enemies.
You know how I am scorned, my shame and my disgrace; all my adversaries are before You.
Scorn has broken my heart, and I am in despair; I looked for sympathy but found none, for comforters but found no one.
They gave me bitter gall for food, and for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Let the table before them become a snare, and a trap for their allies.
Let their eyes be darkened so they cannot see, and make their backs bend forever.
Pour out Your wrath upon them, and let Your fierce anger overtake them.
Let their dwelling be desolate; let no one dwell in their tents.
For they persecute the one You have struck, and add to the pain of those You have wounded.
Add iniquity to their iniquity; let them not enter into Your righteousness.
Let them be blotted out of the Book of Life and not recorded with the righteous.
O God, but I am in pain and distress; let Your salvation protect me on high.
I will praise the name of God with a song, and I will exalt Him with thanksgiving.
This will please the LORD more than an ox, more than a young bull with horns and hooves.
The humble will see and rejoice; you who seek God, may your hearts be revived.
For the LORD listens to the needy and does not despise His people who are imprisoned.
Let heaven and earth praise Him, the seas and everything that moves in them.
For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah, that the people may dwell there and possess it.
The descendants of His servants will inherit it, and those who love His name will settle in it.
